title: Flow of Exception Throwing with 'throw' in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the process of explicitly creating and throwing an exception using the 'throw' keyword, and how it transfers control to the calling method or a catch block.
relationships: Normal execution leads to a condition check
If condition is met, a throw statement creates and throws an exception object
The throw immediately halts the current method
Control moves up the call stack to find a matching catch block
If found, the catch block handles the exception
If no handler is found, the JVM terminates the program
